narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply personal, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam the way it did, based on the details you shared. At the top of what stood out was your Year 1 decile of 1.0, the strongest signal pulling toward a higher risk of not passing the bar, as it placed you at the lower end of your school's performance early on, setting a foundation that the model weighs heavily. Right alongside that, your LSAT score of 36.0 reinforced this concern, signaling to the model a tougher climb through the rigorous bar material ahead. Your Year 3 decile of 1.0 added to that picture, showing the challenge persisted even later in your academic journey. Even with your upper-middle family income, which might suggest some stability, it unfortunately aligned in the model's view with patterns that didn't offset the risks from your grades. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.5 further tipped the scales toward failure in its eyes, painting a story of consistent hurdles in handling demanding coursework. Factors like being a white male in a full-time program also nudged the prediction that way, fitting into broader trends the model has learned from past students. Together, these elements created a 76.9% estimated chance of bar failure that led to this tough call on your application. It's not a judgment on your potential or drive—many have rewritten similar stories with targeted changes. Imagine bolstering those early grades or LSAT prep as your next chapter; what small step could you take today to shift that narrative? You've got resilience in you, and this is just one model's snapshot, not your final verdict. Keep pushing forward.
